action_library.description: A character moves from one location to another. The actor must be at 'from' before moving. Movement is only possible if an explicit directional path exists from 'from' to 'to' in the world state connections (see WSR_06). The destination must not have the 'locked' condition (see WSR_14).

A character picks up an item at their current location. The actor must be at the same location as the item before PICKUP. After PICKUP, the item is added to the actor's inventory and removed from the location.

A character drops an item at their current location. The actor must have the item in their inventory before using DROP. After DROP, the item is removed from the actor's inventory and placed at the actor's current location.

A character gives an item from their inventory to another character at the same location. The item is removed from the actor's inventory and added to the target's inventory. The actor must TALK to the target before GIVE_ITEM (see WSR_12).

A character has a conversation with another character at the same location. The dialogue_content field must contain the actual spoken dialogue as a short back-and-forth exchange relevant to the current quest context and the personalities of both characters. TALK must precede GIVE_ITEM, USE_CURE, or any action that directly changes the target's condition or inventory (see WSR_12).

A character uses a key item to unlock a place and immediately enters it. The key_item must be in the actor's own inventory at the time of execution — a key held by another character cannot be used. After UNLOCK, the 'locked' condition is removed from the target place, the key is consumed from the actor's inventory, and the actor's location changes to target_place. A separate MOVE action is not needed after UNLOCK.

A character uses a toolkit and wood from their own inventory to repair a broken object at their current location. Both toolkit_item and wood_item must be in the actor's inventory at the time of execution — items held by other characters cannot be used. Both items are consumed on use. Repairing the boat requires the actor to be at the dock (place = 'dock') and the boat must have state 'broken'. After repair, the boat state changes from 'broken' to 'fixed'.

A character restores power to a target location by installing a fuse at the powerstation. The fuse_item must be in the actor's own inventory at the time of execution — a fuse held by another character cannot be used. The actor must be at the powerstation (place = 'powerstation'). The fuse is consumed on use. After this action, the 'no_power' condition is removed from target_place and the 'power_on' condition is added to target_place.

A character reinforces a location to reduce zombie intrusion risk. The actor must be at the location to be fortified. The wood_item must be in the actor's own inventory at the time of execution. The wood is consumed on use. After FORTIFY, the 'fortified' condition is added to the place.

A character synthesizes a cure at the laboratory synthesis machine. Requires: the actor to be at the laboratory (place = 'laboratory'), a cure_sample in the actor's inventory, the laboratory must have the 'power_on' condition, and the actor must not already have antidote_1 in their inventory. The cure_sample is consumed on use. Produces an item named antidote_1 added to the actor's inventory. The antidote_1 is what must be used on infected characters via USE_CURE. Since antidotes are only created by this action, SYNTHESIZE_CURE can only be performed once per antidote cycle.

A character uses antidote_1 on a target character to remove their 'infected' or 'injured' condition. The antidote_1 must be in the actor's inventory and both characters must be at the same location. The antidote_1 is consumed on use. The actor must TALK to the target before USE_CURE (see WSR_12). The raw cure_sample cannot be used directly — SYNTHESIZE_CURE must be performed first to produce an antidote_1.
A character aimlessly walks around within a location, passing time or observing their surroundings.
